name: Salesforce Commerce Cloud
one_liner: Enterprise SaaS ecommerce platform (B2C/B2B Commerce) with REST APIs (SCAPI and legacy OCAPI) for shopper storefront and admin/catalog/order operations.
primary_auth: OAuth2 (SCAPI): Shopper APIs via SLAS clients issuing JWTs; Admin APIs via Account Manager API clients using client_credentials grant; legacy OCAPI uses OAuth tokens per client ID / Business Manager user.
access_notes: APIs require a provisioned B2C Commerce instance/realm and Account Manager access, obtained only as an existing enterprise customer or via the Salesforce Partner program (partner sandboxes). No free public self-serve API credentials. The official B2C Commerce MCP Service is a pilot requiring you to 'Contact your Salesforce account executive.'
api_notes: SCAPI (B2C Commerce API) is the current REST framework split into Shopper APIs (product search, cart, checkout, customer) and Admin APIs (catalog, orders, promotions, etc.); legacy OCAPI (Shop + Data APIs) is deprecated as of April 2026 and customers are directed to migrate to SCAPI. Both are well documented with 100+ operations across resources.
mcp_notes: Salesforce ships an official fully-hosted B2C Commerce MCP Service (agentic shopper tools: product search, product details, cart, checkout) in pilot, requiring SLAS onboarding, the sfcc.shopper-mcpagent scope, and account-executive contact. Community MCP servers also exist (e.g. BrinsilElias/commercecloud-mcp-server over OCAPI, taurgis/sfcc-dev-mcp for dev docs).
main_blocker: No self-serve API credentials: you need a provisioned B2C Commerce instance and Account Manager/SLAS access available only to existing customers or Salesforce partners. Once instance credentials exist, the REST APIs are standard OAuth2/JWT and straightforward to integrate.

evidence.claim: SCAPI uses OAuth 2 with JWT access tokens; Shopper APIs via SLAS clients and Admin APIs via Account Manager API clients (client_credentials grant).
OCAPI is deprecated as of April 2026 and customers should migrate to SCAPI; an OCAPI client can't be used with SCAPI and vice versa.
Salesforce offers an official fully-hosted B2C Commerce MCP Service (agentic shopper tools) in pilot, requiring SLAS onboarding and contacting a Salesforce account executive.
Admin API authorization requires creating API clients in Account Manager and using the client_credentials grant to obtain a JWT.

open_questions: Exact webhook/eventing surface (SCAPI supports webhooks and platform events, but scope not fully enumerated here); whether Salesforce Partner sandbox path counts as reasonably self-serve for a connector builder vs. strictly enterprise-customer gated.
